free_text: Elastic neutron scattering from Ca-40 for neutron
energies to 2 MeV
Final paper.
Cross-section data on cards.
spin and l-values, partly suprsdd
- prelim results
Total cross-sect.
Experim.details
Karlsruhe isochronous cyclotron (KIC)
.U-(d,nx) reaction by 40 MeV deuterons.
A deflection system produced neutron bunches of about
1 ns duration at a repetition rate of 19.13 kHz,
neutron flux about 3.e+4 n/cm2*sec.  The neutron beam
was monitored by a pulse-shape discriminator detector.
.The neutron spectrum from the Karlsruhe isochronous
cyclotron spectrometer is a smooth, slowly varying
function in the energy region from 0.55 to 2 MeV.
.A metallic calcium cylinder, 5 cm diameter
and 12 cm long, encapsulated in 50 mu silver foil,
the thickness = 0.09 atoms/barns.
.The sample purity better than 99.5%.
.For normalization to the scattering cross section, a
high purity (99.9%) graphite sample was used in the
form of a hollow cylinder with an inner diam. of 2.5cm
and the same outer dimensions as calcium sample.
Flight path = 58 m, overall time resolution
is 4 ns. (i.e. energy resolution of 700 eV at 0.55 MeV
and 6.1 keV at 2 MeV).
Three NE-102A plastic scintillators, 12.4 cm
diameter by 2.5 cm thick, mounted on XP-1040
photo-multipliers, and fast anode output had a zero
crossover tunnel diode time mark circuit.
.As the neutron flux detector, a small NE-102A
scintillator (5 cm diameter, 1.2 cm thick) was used.
.The system (4 NE-102A detectors, a detector position
coder and Laben UC-KB time analyser) coupled to a
CDC-3100 on-line computer.
.The TOF spectrum for each detector was taken over a
range of 4 microsec with channel widths of 2ns.
.A neutron beam monitor placed at 6 deg to the neutron
beam at a distance of 11 m from the source.
.By aluminum collimator, 30 cm in diameter by 1 m long,
the neutron beam confined to 3.0 micro-steradian at
the end of the 58 m flight path.
.Data were simultaneously taken at laboratory angles of
54, 90 and 140 degrees by 3 scattering detectors, and
0 deg for flux monitoring (NE-102A).
.Corrections were made for the finite scattering sample
thickness of Ca and C measured with similar energy
resolution at the KIC spectrometer, in the calculation
of the cross section.  The correction was small for
thin Ca sample but was about 20% for C data,
no corrections were made for multiple scattering.
.Back ground counting rates in the open beam spectrum
were less than 5% of the sample out counting
rate above 1 MeV. Below this energy the fraction
increased to 15%.
.The primary purpose of the work was to assign spins
and parities (l-values) for Ca40(n,n) resonances by
the resonance shapes and symmetry properties.  Precise
absolute values for the Ca-40 differential scattering
cross section were not the principal concern.
n-widths, aver. Res. Pars added.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
BIB updated. ERR-ANALYS moved to Subents 002-004.
REL-REF added.
.The differential neutron scattering cross section from
0.55 to 2.0 MeV is given with high energy resolution,
the first inelastic channel opens at 3.35 MeV. For a
lower threshold (200 keV neutrons) of the detector
and a short sample-detector distance (24.4 cm),
inelastically scattered neutrons are not detected.
The data agree
well but direct comparison is difficult because of the
widely different resolution functions.
The statistical error is given as 3% and
after normalizing to the carbon, the
absolute cross section values are estimated to  have
less than 15% uncertainty.
Private communication from Nebe 4/12/1970.
SD: REL-REF added. STATUS updated.
ERR-ANALYS added from Subent 001. DATA-ERR -> ERR-T.
ERR-S added.

SD: STATUS updated.
.R-matrix formalism is applied and formula by Blatt and
Biedenharn are used to calculating the angular
dependence of scattering and reaction processes.
.The method of shape identification used here requires
some knowledge of the background scattering (i.e.
channel radius and potential scattering).
.The assignments of resonance spin and parity have
utilized 3 general features of the differential cross-
section such as,(1) the resonance shape, (2) the
amplitude behavior as function of scattering angle,
(3) the peak position shift.
Taken from Nucl.Phys.,A154(1970)36.

.The angular momentum l is based on the shape analysis
of differential cross section and on the total cross
section results of KFK-1000.
.parity = (-)**l.
